title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: Reproducing published population pharmacokinetic / pharmacodynamic (popPK/PD) model executable form deceptively hard information-extraction problem. source corpus spans roughly four decades, multiple modelling tools notational traditions, enforced reporting standard. describe nlmixr2libingest, tooling lets large-language-model agent extract models nlmixr2lib library scale holding strict quality invariant: every estimated value every nonstandard structural equation final model source-traced original paper. frame cost agentic extraction (dominated cached-context re-reads across build–check–fix loop, paper), catalogue heterogeneity makes extraction hard, present deterministic-first architecture — log filtering, register lookup, structural archetypes, optional local-LLM distillation — whose every acceleration cheaper input prior, never gate agent reads paper.

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: Population PK/PD models published mix prose, equations, tables, (sometimes) control streams. reader wants run model must recover compartmental structure, parameterisation, inter-individual residual variability, covariate relationships, dosing/observation conventions, re-express modelling language. faithfully one paper routine expert work; thousands papers industrial-scale extraction problem unusual constraint — output must numerically faithful fixed ground truth (paper), “plausible wrong” failure, minor error. LLM agents can extraction, cost structure unintuitive: paper cheap (trimmed paper order 8.6k tokens); expense cached context re-read every turn build–check–fix loop, multiplied number turns. Reducing cost therefore means shrinking per-turn context, cutting turn count, dispatching agent large fraction indexed documents fittable popPK papers. central design tension cheapest way shrink context — letting retrieval system small model decide agent sees — exactly way silently lose fidelity. resolution quality firewall: cheap pre-processing may propose prioritise, agent always verifies final numbers nonstandard equations full paper, never gated.

what: 2. The heterogeneity problem (why this is hard)
title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: Notes toward taxonomy varies across corpus. item concrete extraction hazard, just trivia. standardised reporting. enforced equivalent CONSORT popPK. Structural model, parameter table, variability model, covariate equations may text, table, figure, appendix, supplementary control stream. Tooling notation drift ~40 years. NONMEM (ADVAN/TRANS macros, THETA/ETA/EPS), Monolix (mlxtran), ADAPT, WinNonlin, SAAM II, Phoenix, Stan, hand-derived closed-form solutions appear. model can written ODEs, closed-form linCmt()-style solution, ADVAN subroutine references reader must expand. Parameterisation ambiguity. Clearance/volume vs micro-rate constants; CL/V vs k/V; absorption first-order ka, transit chains, zero-order; central volume reported V, V1, Vc, V/F. Apparent (/F) vs absolute parameters frequently implicit. Variability conventions. IIV reported variance, SD, CV%, %; log, logit, additive scale; full vs block vs diagonal Ω; shrinkage sometimes conflated estimates. Residual error additive, proportional, combined, log-normal, units must inferred. Units scaling. Mixed time units (h vs day), amount vs concentration, molar vs mass, allometric reference weights left unstated, BSA/FFM/IBW derivations (DuBois/Mosteller, Janmahasatian, Devine) often uncited. Covariate model expression. Centering references, fixed vs estimated exponents, indicator-variable encodings, study-specific covariates whose meaning portable across papers (motivation scope field covariate register). Document quality. Older papers scanned images requiring OCR; tables break across pages; Greek letters subscripts mangled; errata supplements live separate files. Non-fittable look-alikes. GastroPlus/PBPK-QSP exports, posters, reviews, methods papers textually similar fittable popPK papers dispatched extraction.

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: treat per-run cost cache_read × output, cache_read roughly (cached context size) × (number agent turns). optimisation targets therefore () per-turn context size, (b) turn count, (c) share dispatches spent non-fittable papers. Every mechanism admissible firewall Section 1: source-trace every final ini() value nonstandard model() equation; never gate paper; treat machine outputs priors.

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: Log filtering. devtools::check() vignette-render logs reduced deterministically ERROR/WARNING/NOTE steps failing chunks. Determinism point: LLM summary drops real error corrupt loop. Register lookup. three nlmixr2lib canonical-name registers (covariate ≈284k tokens, compartment, parameter) parsed DuckDB full-text index; agent retrieves relevant entries name, alias, concept instead reading file. index rebuilds source register changes. Structural features archetypes. existing model parsed (via rxUi) structural feature vector — compartments, absorption, elimination, PD type, variability, covariates, residual error — augmented description/reference/vignette text. Clustering yields empirical archetype taxonomy, explicit bucket unique--interesting structures flagged rather forced template.

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: local LLM configured, embeddings (nomic-embed-text) drive fittable classifier archetype prior, small instruct model emits structured distillation sheet (parameters, values, units, RSE, IIV, covariate equations, residual error, dosing) agent builds verifies . LLM, classification falls back keyword/feature rules distillation skipped — agent reads trimmed paper directly. acceleration never load-bearing correctness.

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: pre-dispatch classifier annotates likely-non-fittable papers sets effort tier (annotate-first; auto-skip held-validation). soft, complexity-tiered budget checkpoints work escalates human via sidecar question overrun rather hard-killing.

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: Dollar figures use runner’s recorded blended rate (~$1.3/Mtok-total; cost dominated cache-read). Token figures re-runs recovered agents’ per-message usage transcripts.

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: Two original library extractions, runner’s complete RunRecords: vedolizumab 3.25M tokens (2.86M cache-read, 23k output), $4.27, ~20 turns; van Dijkman decline 1.79M tokens (1.54M cache-read), $2.67, 5 attempts. ~96% tokens — cost — cache reads. Empirically, run cost ≈ rate × Σ_turns(cached context), two control variables per-turn context size number turns.

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: Re-extracting four already-curated papers source alone, cross-checking shipped models papers: () 1-cmt linear mAb — faithful, 11/11 values source-traced, actually corrected unit error shipped model (paper reports ng/mL; curated model used µg/mL, 1000× simulation); (ii) 2-cmt parallel-linear+Michaelis-Menten model 13 covariates — full structural numeric match, 31/31 traced (incl. supplement’s Table S4); (iii) PK-free algebraic exposure-response PD model — correct structure, one real error (used residual variance 7.09 SD 2.66 belongs); (iv) meta-modelling catalogue 11 cited models — correctly declined fabricate, matching operator’s original deselection. Two value disagreements, one direction, resolved reading paper. judgement-versus-mechanism boundary real: model structure values extract reliably; unit conventions variance-vs-SD encodings errors hide.

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: programmatic source-trace flags ini() values supporting number paper, verifies presence, slot: erroneous residual 7.09 read “found” number paper — variance. Catching right-number-wrong-role error still requires reading labelled column. role-aware LLM-cross-checked variant future work.

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: six-covariate task, two ways, full transcript telemetry: reading covariate-columns.md (~136k tokens) context cost 17.6M tokens / $22.84 (peak per-turn context 532k); resolving six via lookup_canonical cost 0.47M tokens / $0.62 (peak context 44k) — ~37× cheaper, 12× smaller peak context, identical answers. lookup strict win agent otherwise load register.

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: Verbose, general-purpose re-extractions cost 5–14× originals ($41 vs $4.27 hard case) — register (lookup-capable agents never loaded ; peak context stayed file size). driver turn count: 126–267 model turns versus tuned runner’s ~20. cost ≈ context × turns, ~10× turn inflation produced ~10× cost inflation. single correctly-declined case (23 turns) one matched original’s cost.

what: 4.6 Supervised A/B on the live runner: the levers in a real extraction
title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: Section 4.4 measured one lever isolation; combined token-minimising skill measured end--end production skill real dispatch runner, full RunRecord telemetry (accurate vehicle earlier ad-hoc probes lacked). Two skills compared: production extract-literature-model -lean variant () resolves every canonical covariate/parameter name one batched pre-brief instead lookup per name, (ii) replaces separate vignette-render, convention-lint, R CMD check gates single validate_model(level = "full") call. Everything else held fixed — two papers, model (claude-opus-4-7), effort, one task time single account, byte-identical prompts except skill name — cell run triplicate. Aside (baseline provenance, later detailed write-): production skill extract-literature-model nlmixr2lib commit dc10fec8 (origin/main experiment time); lean variant committed 2226ba8f. per-run figures re-derived per-attempt result events (authoritative totals claude reports), runner’s summary RunRecord, one attempt’s RunRecord proved unreliable (see ). One run excluded: easy/lean/1, complete extraction output-evidence gate wrongly flagged “output” task neither committed declared deliverable file — harness-config error, since corrected, model failure. second run, hard/production/2, first looked anomalous (RunRecord showed 102 output tokens) fact real, expensive run included: attempt held three claude invocations (81-turn extraction plus two resumes), RunRecord captured last resume’s per-call usage cost ($9.74) cumulative; result events render-loop extraction 38k output / 11.9M cache-read. Every individual extraction run ($0 stop-sequence throttle-aborts precede runs omitted; * = excluded summary): * easy/lean/1 (gate-failed task) also ran second complete extraction (run 4: $1.66 / 1.23M total) killed; excluded harness-config artifact, model failure. Means / medians included runs (cost | cache-read): easy production $2.86 / $2.63 | 2.32M vs lean $1.76 / $1.76 | 1.32M; hard production $6.47 / $5.72 | 7.35M vs lean $5.05 / $4.89 | 6.27M. headline lean skill cheaper papers — clearly simple model, modestly noisily complex one. easy model cut cost ~38 % (−43 % cache-read), mainly collapsing several per-name lookups one batched pre-brief passing validation one two tries. hard model lean ~22 % cheaper mean (~14 % median) less cache-read — gap driven largely variance: production’s worst run fell render-loop cost $9.74 (expensive run set), whereas lean’s three runs consistent ($4.13–6.13). Per-run turn counts hard model comparable (production ~58, lean ~57). honest reading “cheaper--comparable lower-variance hard,” large guaranteed win, fragile hard vignette can trigger render-loop blow-either skill. wall-clock caveat orthogonal tokens: lean ~2× slower hard model unified validate_model(level = "full") re-runs whole-package R CMD check plus render every fix iteration — minutes subprocess time costs little tokens much latency. Scoping validator new model alone remove latency without giving token savings.

what: 4.7 Where the tokens go across the production corpus
title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: Sampling 322 completed production extractions (production skill; cost range $0.40–$41.87, median $1.84) confirms generalises Section 4.5. Almost spend cache-read — agent’s accumulated context (skill + references + paper + model/vignette edit + every prior tool result) re-read turn — scales turn count: distribution heavily right-skewed: runs converge handful turns stay cheap, small tail explodes. tail almost always vignette-render fix loop — $41.87 run issued 105 render calls, expensive runs carry high render counts many large (>10k-token) tool results. failed render adds turn, appends large render log context, forces whole growing context re-read every subsequent turn. biggest lever total corpus spend per-turn context (already modest 284k register replaced lookup) capping preventing render loop — precisely failure mode lean skill’s unified validator must careful amplify (Section 4.6).

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: levers package aimed correctly — per-turn context (register lookup, log filtering, lean lazily-loaded skill) — save real money triggered (lookup: ~37× isolated task). dominant cost driver practice number turns, property dispatching agent’s discipline tooling. practical conclusion biggest savings come tuned, low-turn dispatch (production runner already ), input-side levers compounding top. Several input-side pre-processing steps follow directly cost model candidates low-turn path: cheap local-LLM pass reads paper, identifies covariates, emits focused, model-specific covariate report (paper name → canonical name/units/reference) agent neither loads register spends turns looking covariates ; single code-based validation step runs parse, conventions, source-trace, registry-build, check, vignette-render returns one terse “Success” short list file--line fixes, replacing verbose logs otherwise bloat every subsequent turn; policy-driven sidecar auto-responder answers recurring stop--ask questions vetted policy table next tick, run continues warm cache instead paying cold retry. keeps expensive judgement agent moving deterministic pre-decidable work per-turn critical path. Open limitations remain: source-trace presence-vs-slot gap ; portability cost local-LLM dependency (mitigated graceful degradation); reliance small local models distillation, source-trace firewall designed catch; boundary discipline ingestion package validated library feeds, must stay one-directional (consume, never reimplement validation).

title: Ingesting four decades of population-PK literature: challenges and a token-efficient, quality-preserving pipeline
text: token-efficient extraction pipeline need trade away fidelity. keeping every acceleration input prior side hard source-trace firewall, making LLM optional rather central, deterministic core lowers cost also keeps agent honest.

what: Token-Efficient Ingestion Tooling for the nlmixr2lib Model Library
title: Token-Efficient Ingestion Tooling for the nlmixr2lib Model Library
text: Token-efficient, quality-preserving tooling ingesting published population-PK/PD models nlmixr2lib scale. cost agentic literature-model extraction dominated cache_read × output tokens across build→check→fix loop — paper . package attacks cost without weakening quality firewall: agent still source-traces every final ini() value nonstandard model()-block equation original paper. Every feature cheaper input prior — never gate agent sees paper.

title: Token-Efficient Ingestion Tooling for the nlmixr2lib Model Library
text: nlmixr2libingest ingestion . consumes nlmixr2lib — canonical-name registers, compiled model database, rxUi parse, authoring API — never reimplements model validation, stays nlmixr2lib::checkModelConventions().

title: Token-Efficient Ingestion Tooling for the nlmixr2lib Model Library
text: Output filtering — filter_check_log() / filter_render_log() inst/scripts/rcheck.sh deterministically reduce devtools::check() / vignette-render logs ERROR/WARNING/NOTE lines failing chunks (deterministic purpose: dropped error quality regression). Register lookup — lookup_canonical() inst/scripts/lookup.R: DuckDB full-text index three nlmixr2lib registers returns relevant entries (~1–2k tokens) instead reading ~284k-token covariate file. index rebuilds source register changes (size/mtime/md5), per-session TTL. Structural features & archetypes — model_features() / build_feature_table() parse every library model structural feature table; assign_archetype() / archetype_taxonomy() / archetype_template() derive interpretable archetype taxonomy (explicit bucket) starting templates; cluster_features() gives data-driven cross-check. Fittable / archetype classifier — build_store() / classify() labelled corpus (TF-IDF always; embeddings local LLM available, via embed_text()), predicting whether paper fittable popPK/PD model top-archetype prior. Outputs priors annotate-first screening, auto-decisions. Source-trace pre-check — source_trace() searches paper supporting number final ini() value (back-transform- rounding-tolerant) flags none, plus structural model() equations hardcoded constants verify. assists, never replaces, mandatory manual source-trace. Soft budget backstop — complexity_tier() / budget_advisor() give complexity-scaled, gracefully-escalating token budget (continue → checkpoint → checkpoint--escalate, never hard kill); run_token_stats() summarises runner’s per-run token/cost records. Distillation — distill_paper() produces advisory structured extraction sheet via local LLM (LLM-optional; NULL backend). Unified validation — validate_model() runs whole chain (parse → checkModelConventions() → source_trace(), plus optional R CMD check filtered vignette render) returns one terse Success / fix-list instead multi-thousand-line logs otherwise re-cache every turn. CLI: inst/scripts/validate.R (exit 0 = success, 1 = issues). Naming pre-brief — naming_prebrief() resolves paper’s covariates (optionally parameters/compartments) canonical names , front, agent ingests one small report instead loading ~136k-token register looking terms turn--turn. Deterministic register scan ∪ optional LLM augmentation; prior, gate. CLI: inst/scripts/prebrief.R. Sidecar policy responder — sidecar_respond() auto-answers rule-like runner stop--ask pauses vetted policy table (inst/policy/sidecar-policy.yaml, mined 1760 sidecar records) run continues warm cache instead cold retry. ever selects option agent already offered, fires single policy, escalates everything judgement-dependent human; every auto-answer audited. CLI: inst/scripts/sidecar_respond.R.

title: Token-Efficient Ingestion Tooling for the nlmixr2lib Model Library
text: Hard dependencies CRAN-(DBI, duckdb, cli, jsonlite). local-LLM features (embedding-based classification, distillation) optional degrade gracefully — LLM configured, deterministic levers (log filtering, register lookup, features/archetypes, source-trace) still run classification falls back keyword matching.

title: Token-Efficient Ingestion Tooling for the nlmixr2lib Model Library
text: Phases 0–3 plus three input-side levers (unified validation, naming pre-brief, sidecar policy responder) implemented tested (devtools::check clean). remaining work integration — wiring naming_prebrief() / lookup_canonical(), validate_model() / rcheck.sh, source_trace() extract-literature-model skill; fittable classifier runner’s pre-dispatch hook; sidecar_respond.R runner’s sidecar watcher.

title: Token-Efficient Ingestion Tooling for the nlmixr2lib Model Library
text: See inst/design/ARCHITECTURE.md engineering design vignettes/articles/ingestion-challenges.Rmd methods-paper narrative ingesting four decades non-standardised popPK literature.

what: Look up canonical names in the nlmixr2lib registers — lookup_canonical
title: Look up canonical names in the nlmixr2lib registers — lookup_canonical
text: Returns handful register entries relevant term without ever loading multi-hundred-thousand-token register files context. Exact name alias matches surfaced first, full-text (BM25) token matches. long example_models list summarised count default.

title: Look up canonical names in the nlmixr2lib registers — lookup_canonical
code: lookup_canonical(   term,   kind = NULL,   top_k = 5,   full = FALSE,   db_path = register_db_path(),   dir = registers_dir() )

title: Look up canonical names in the nlmixr2lib registers — lookup_canonical
text: term Search term: canonical name, alias, free-text concept (e.g. "body weight"). kind Optionally restrict "covariate", "compartment", "parameter". top_k Maximum rows return. full TRUE, include full example_models list text. db_path, dir Index location register directory.

title: Look up canonical names in the nlmixr2lib registers — lookup_canonical
text: data frame matching entries (0 rows none match), ordered relevance.

what: Build a model-specific naming pre-brief from a paper — naming_prebrief
title: Build a model-specific naming pre-brief from a paper — naming_prebrief
text: Resolves covariates (optionally parameters/compartments) paper uses canonical nlmixr2lib names front, agent ingests one small report instead loading register looking terms turn--turn. Candidates come deterministic register scan plus, local LLM configured, distill_paper(); resolved lookup_canonical(). Unmatched candidates (possible new canonical names) flagged. prior, gate: agent still source-traces every value paper.

title: Build a model-specific naming pre-brief from a paper — naming_prebrief
code: naming_prebrief(   paper,   kinds = "covariate",   backend = .llmBackend(),   top_k = 3L,   dir = registers_dir(),   db_path = register_db_path() )

title: Build a model-specific naming pre-brief from a paper — naming_prebrief
text: paper Paper text, path (trimmed) paper file. kinds register kinds resolve: "covariate", "parameter", "compartment". backend LLM backend candidate-augmenting distillation ("ollama"/"none"/"auto"); "none" uses deterministic scan . top_k Rows consider per lookup (best used). dir Register directory (defaults registers_dir()). db_path Lookup index location (defaults register_db_path()).

title: Build a model-specific naming pre-brief from a paper — naming_prebrief
text: nli_prebrief list: one entry per kind (matched/unmatched), plus backend n (matched count). Render render_prebrief().

what: Programmatic source-trace pre-check for a model against its paper — source_trace
title: Programmatic source-trace pre-check for a model against its paper — source_trace
text: every estimated ini() value, searches paper text supporting number (back-transform- rounding-tolerant) flags values none. Also lists structural model() equations hardcoded constants verify. assists – never replaces – mandatory manual source-trace.

Programmatic source-trace pre-check for a model against its paper — source_trace
text: model rxUi, model function, nlmixr2lib model name. paper Paper text, path (trimmed) paper file. tol Relative tolerance numeric match (default 0.05 = 5%).

title: Programmatic source-trace pre-check for a model against its paper — source_trace
text: nli_sourcetrace list ini (per-parameter trace), equations (per-line model() classification), summary counts.

what: Validate a model (and optionally its vignette) into one terse result — validate_model
title: Validate a model (and optionally its vignette) into one terse result — validate_model
text: Runs whole validation chain returns compact pass/fix object instead verbose logs otherwise re-cache every turn build-check-fix loop. fast tier (parse, conventions, source-trace) pure R meant iteration; full tier adds R CMD check vignette render. Convention checking delegated nlmixr2lib::checkModelConventions() – function never reimplements .

title: Validate a model (and optionally its vignette) into one terse result — validate_model
code: validate_model(   model,   paper = NULL,   level = c("fast", "full"),   pkg = NULL,   vignette = NULL,   tol = 0.05 )

title: Validate a model (and optionally its vignette) into one terse result — validate_model
text: model rxUi, model function, nlmixr2lib model name. paper Optional paper text path; enables source-trace stage. level "fast" (parse + conventions + source-trace) "full" (adds R CMD check vignette render). pkg Package directory full-tier R CMD check. vignette Optional path model's validation vignette render. tol Relative tolerance source-trace numeric matches.

title: Validate a model (and optionally its vignette) into one terse result — validate_model
text: nli_validation object: status ("success"/"issues"), issues (data frame), counts, stages run.
